title: The Art of Oathbreaking
subtitle: keep your promises, pay your dues
footer: An offical author's archive to keep me sane.

about.body:
The Art of Oathbreaking follows a violet-haired outsider who drifts through the lives of the powerful and the dangerous, picking up their buried secrets the way other men pick up loose change. He never meant to keep them. He just has the kind of face people confess to.

Born under a circus tent, raised on the high wire, he traded the trapeze for a slackline, the slackline for a tightrope, and finally the rope for a motorcycle — becoming the most famous stunt rider of his decade under a name that wasn't his. The secrets he carries belong to demigods, criminals, and stranger things. The one he can't recover is his own.

This archive collects the characters, the timeline, the relationships, and the lore. Spoilers are blurred where it matters. Open Edit to make it yours.

refs.label: Tone & Influences
refs.note: Dark carnival; 1960s–2000s; secrets-as-currency; found family that frays.

refs.label: Aesthetic Board
refs.note: Violet, brass, sawdust, chrome. Replace this link with your moodboard.

characters.name: Cassius Veil
characters.excerpt: He never asked to know. People simply told him, the way you tell a wall.
characters.summary: Trapeze child, tightrope prodigy, then the stunt motorcyclist whose name filled arenas. Cassius moves between the civilian world and the criminal one without belonging to either — which is exactly why the powerful trust him with what they'd kill to hide. The violet hair he was born with is the one thing about his past he can't explain. By the story's end, neither can anyone else.

fields.val: Carrying secrets he was never meant to keep

characters.name: Iolanthe Marrow
characters.excerpt: Every favour she grants is a thread. Pull enough of them and a man unravels.
characters.summary: A demigod who runs her empire on promises rather than money. Iolanthe never threatens; she lends. The debt always comes due, and it always costs more than it was worth. She takes an interest in Cassius the moment she learns what he is — and she may have known his mother.

characters.name: Thackeray Bell
characters.excerpt: He gave the boy a name, a wire, and one rule: never tell what you're told.
characters.summary: The ringmaster who took in an abandoned violet-haired infant and raised him in the only home Cassius ever trusted. Thackeray taught him balance, performance, and the discipline of silence. He knows more about where Cassius came from than he ever admits.

fields.val: The wire, the show, the silence

characters.name: Wren
characters.excerpt: She isn't here to protect him. She's here to make sure he doesn't find out too soon.
characters.summary: An earthling fixer who works the border between the mortal world and the demigods' games. Wren is assigned to Cassius — to watch, to steer, occasionally to save. Her loyalties are a question the story keeps asking.

characters.name: Dovan Crale
characters.excerpt: Second-best for ten years, and he never forgave the violet-haired ghost for it.
characters.summary: A stunt rider who spent a decade losing the marquee to Six-Spoke. When the criminal world comes looking for leverage on Cassius, Dovan is happy to point the way.

timeline.title: Born Under the Big Top
timeline.body: An infant with violet hair is left at the door of Bell's Travelling Wonder. Thackeray names him Cassius and tells no one where he came from — possibly because he doesn't know.

timeline.title: First Wire Walk
timeline.body: At eight he crosses the high wire without a net. The crowd gasps; Thackeray never lets him do it again that way. The boy has found the only place the noise in his head goes quiet.

timeline.title: The Slackline Season
timeline.body: Restless on the rigid wire, he learns the slackline — the discipline of holding still on something that won't. It teaches him patience he'll later mistake for peace.

timeline.title: Trading Rope for Throttle
timeline.body: The circus is dying. Cassius trades the tightrope for a motorcycle and reinvents himself for the arenas as 'Six-Spoke' — a rider with no listed hometown and impossible balance.

timeline.title: The Devil's Loop
timeline.body: He lands the stunt no one survives twice. Overnight he's the most famous rider of the decade — and visible enough that the powerful start to notice the man who keeps perfect silence.

timeline.title: A Patron's Favour
timeline.body: Iolanthe Marrow extends a single favour after a near-fatal crash. Cassius takes it. He doesn't yet understand that her favours are threads, and that she has just sewn the first stitch.

timeline.title: The First Oath
timeline.body: A dying crime-lord whispers a secret to Cassius instead of a priest. It's the first of many. He becomes the place powerful people put what they cannot bury — and the threads tighten.

timeline.title: What the Violet Hid
timeline.body: Chasing one last secret, Cassius finally reaches his own. The hair, the silence, the way the powerful confess to him — none of it was ever an accident. The oath he was born to break is his.

lore.name: The Oath-Debt
lore.summary: Oaths are real, and they hold weight. To swear one is to owe one. The powerful trade them like currency — and a secret confessed is a kind of oath laid on the listener. Cassius has been collecting that weight his whole life without knowing the bill exists.

lore.name: Demigods, Humans & Earthlings
lore.summary: Three kinds of people move through this world. Humans, who forget. Demigods, who remember too much and outlive their oaths. And earthlings — older, quieter, bound to the ground in ways no one explains aloud. Cassius is filed as human. The file is wrong.

lore.name: The Border
lore.summary: Not a place — a profession. Border-walkers move between the civilian world and the criminal one, between mortals and demigods, carrying what can't cross openly. Wren is one. Cassius, without ever being recruited, is another.

fields.val: To the crossing, not the sides

notes.title: The face people confess to
notes.body: Establish early that Cassius's defining trait isn't the hair or the stunts — it's that strangers tell him things. Plant it as charm. Pay it off as horror.

notes.title: Iolanthe's favour
notes.body: The crash that puts him in her debt should look like luck. Reader shouldn't clock the stitch until much later.

casting.title: Cassius Veil
casting.castingNote: Lean, watchful, moves like he's still on a wire. The violet should read as natural, never costume.
casting.authorNote: If you've ever seen someone go very still in a loud room — that's him.

casting.title: Iolanthe Marrow
casting.castingNote: Warmth that costs you something. Never raises her voice.
casting.authorNote: She smiles before she lends. Always before.

scrapbook.quote: I don't keep secrets. I keep the people who give them to me.
A favour is the politest cage ever built.

sectionIntros.characters: The drifter, the lender, the ringmaster, and the people the secrets belong to.
sectionIntros.timeline: Four decades, from the big top to the day the violet-haired man met his own past. Spoilers are blurred — tap to reveal.
sectionIntros.notes: The lore that holds the world up, and the drafting notes that hold the story together.
sectionIntros.tree: Family is a loose word here. These are the bonds that did the raising.
sectionIntros.casting: A wall of faces and lines — flip a card for the casting and author notes.
sectionIntros.links: Who owes whom. The whole story is in the threads.
